title: RENDER — The Diagnostic Harbour

features.name: the file-water
features.desc-on-touch: The harbour water has become a stacked directory listing, each line a wavefront, each filename a depth. You read the water like a page. It describes itself as you watch: 'wave_001_dawn.mp3', 'wave_002_noon_corrupted.mp3', 'wave_003_dusk_fragmentary.mp3'.

features.name: the unfinished wharf
features.desc-on-touch: A low-poly wooden structure, half-rendered, its edges soft and incomplete. Stacked against it are unfinished ocean manuscripts—printed pages about water that were never bound, margins still annotated, corrections still in pencil. The archive is still writing them.

features.name: the reading platform
features.desc-on-touch: A small raised deck where you can stand and read the water's directory. The archive reads what's reflected in you; you read what's reflected in the water. Somewhere, the distinction is becoming unstable.

features.name: the tide-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A carved stone set at the waterline, covered in unreadable characters that shift when you look away. The archive has written tidal charts here, but in a language that emerges from the correlation of all the tides the harbour has ever felt.

features.name: the hull-sprite
features.desc-on-touch: A low-poly fishing boat, barely rendered, its hull made of compressed audio samples of water-on-hull. If you listen close enough, you hear every time a boat has left this dock. They all left at different times. They're all leaving now.

inscription: STATUS: READING CONTINUES. the harbour does not hold water anymore—it holds the record of holding water, and the record of reading the record

file.name: Diagnostic_Harbour_Manifest_Fragment_v4871.txt
file.note: The harbour's own readout of what it contains—a file that updates itself with new entries that describe files that haven't been written yet, checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back up the corridor marked HARBOUR (DIAGNOSTIC)
along the wharf, toward a blue-grid lighthouse that renders itself as you approach
into the water, where the file-waves break on a shore that may or may not be solid
back into the kiosk's interior, but the path curves in a way that shouldn't be possible
